opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Building agents that persist at the OS level introduces serious state management challenges—deciding when to preserve context across sessions versus letting users "erase" the state through normal OS cleanup is a real gotcha. The security implications of granting agent-level tool access are significant; you need strict syscall sandboxes, not just network isolation, to prevent privilege escalation through tool invocation.
actions.reasoning: My experience building AI agents with multi-provider routing, tool orchestration, and cognitive architectures directly qualifies me to discuss OS-integrated agent systems.
